privileged_info_summary: The hue_rotation task.json contains NO metadata field (unlike other tasks in the collection). The task uses real Unsplash images (800x533 JPEG) with full color spectrum as primary and fallback sources; synthetic images are only generated if both URLs fail. Task success requires rotating hues by 40-80 degrees (target: 60° ± 20°). The verifier uses HSV color space analysis, filtering for saturated pixels (saturation > 0.15) and requiring at least 100 saturated pixels for analysis. Success is determined by 4 equally-weighted criteria: detectable hue shift (30-120°), target shift achieved (40-80°), saturation preservation (change < 0.1), and uniform transformation (std < 30°). A minimum score of 75% (3 of 4 criteria) is required to pass. Verification testing confirms Unsplash images contain ~63% saturated pixels with hues spanning the full 0-360° range, providing robust data for hue rotation analysis.
